name: Whole Wheat Croissants
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine yeast and warm water in a bowl. Stir to dissolve the yeast. Let sit about 10 minutes.
2. Gradually add half of the flour, then water, and honey. Whisk until smooth. Cover with a towel and let stand for 1 1/2 hours.
3. In another bowl, combine remaining flour with butter and flatten the butter pieces using your hands.
4. Pour yeast batter into the flour mixture and fold together gently with a spatula. Just moisten the flour without breaking the butter pieces.
5. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Pat it down and roll into an 18-inch x 12-inch rectangle. If sticky, sprinkle with additional flour.
6. Use a metal spatula to fold one-third of the dough towards the center. Then fold from each side, covering the first third with another one-third. Lift the folded dough off the work surface and scrape it clean.
7. Sprinkle the work area with flour and repeat rolling and folding 3 more times. The dough should be firm; if not, freeze for 45 minutes.
8. Pat the dough into a rectangle. Cut into portions for the desired number of croissants (e.g., for 24 croissants: cut in half lengthwise, then each half into three pieces, resulting in 6 portions).
9. Work with one portion at a time, keeping others refrigerated until ready to use. Roll each piece individually into 5-inch x 14-inch rectangles.
10. Cut each rectangle into two 5-inch x 7-inch pieces. Cut each piece diagonally to form four triangles; roll from the wide end to the point.
11. Place on ungreased cookie sheets, spacing them a few inches apart. Curve the ends to create crescent shapes.
12. Beat egg with water and brush croissants lightly. Let them rest for 1 hour, then brush again with the egg mixture.
13. Bake at 375°F until puffed and golden brown. Allow to cool slightly before serving.
